title: Study for a Quiz
summary: Review material effectively for a short quiz using focused techniques.
learnerPromise: You will be able to prepare for a short quiz using focused review methods that help you remember key material.
whyItMatters: Studying with a plan helps you remember more in less time than just rereading. Focused review is one of the best ways to do well on quizzes.
realLifeUses: Reviewing vocabulary words before a language quiz
Practicing math problems before a short test
Going over notes before a science class quiz
Using flashcards to memorize dates for a history quiz
result: A completed 20-minute study session with a self-check of what you learned
youWillLearn: How to pick out the most important material to study
How to test yourself instead of just rereading
How to use short focused blocks of time for review
How to check if you are ready for a quiz
miniChallenge: Spend 20 minutes reviewing material for an upcoming quiz. Cover your notes and try to say the key facts out loud or write them from memory.
steps: Gather your notes and identify the topics the quiz will cover.
Break the material into small chunks and pick one to start with.
Read a section, then cover it and try to say the key points out loud.
Switch to a new chunk and repeat until you have reviewed all topics.
Do a final self-check by listing everything you remember without looking.
completionCriteria: You reviewed all the quiz topics in focused blocks
You tested yourself by recalling facts without looking at notes
You can list most of the key points from memory
commonProblems: Rereading notes over and over without testing yourself
Studying for too long without a break and losing focus
Trying to memorize everything instead of focusing on key points
tips: Self-testing works better than rereading the same material
Study in a quiet place and put your phone out of reach
Sleep helps memory, so study the night before if you can
